fingerprints: Next.js App Router is in use rather than a Pages Router-only deployment
GET /{app_route}?_rsc={cache_buster} with RSC headers returns text/x-component or another React Flight response
POST Server Action fetches are handled on page routes via the next-action header
signals: Responses show text/x-component, _rsc cache-busting behavior, or x-nextjs-* headers associated with App Router traffic
Malformed or invalid Server Action probes trigger Next.js-specific action parsing errors or x-nextjs-action-not-found
The deployment has not yet been upgraded to 15.0.5 / 15.1.9 / 15.2.6 / 15.3.6 / 15.4.8 / 15.5.7 / 16.0.7 or later
verification_summary: GET /{app_route}?_rsc={cache_buster} with RSC and Next-Router-State-Tree headers and confirm a text/x-component or React Flight response
POST /{app_route} with a benign body and a candidate next-action header to observe Next.js Server Action handling without using a weaponized payload
exploitation_summary: POST to a reachable App Router route with next-action / RSC headers and a crafted React flight body for a low-noise command-execution probe
